mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, the Federal Reserve maintains a restrictive monetary stance that keeps real rates elevated. This suppresses equity valuations and increases demand for safe-haven Treasury bonds, driving 10-year yields downward as investors rotate into fixed income. The mechanism operates continuously in high-rate regimes.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 55% of 20-day forward windows when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling period in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE increase > 0.3pp over 3 months) signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers flight-to-quality flows into Treasuries, compressing yields as investors demand safe assets. The signal activates only when unemployment acceleration exceeds the threshold.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession phase
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and 20-day yield direction is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 330) signals demand-driven inflation that forces the Fed to maintain or raise rates. Higher real rates compress equity multiples and increase Treasury yields as the market prices in persistent monetary tightness.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 102.0 while unemployment remains elevated, indicating supply-side weakness and stagflationary pressure. This regime forces the Fed to choose between inflation control and growth support, typically resulting in yield volatility but upward pressure as inflation concerns dominate.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to outperform single-series INDPRO or UNRATE signals (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 1400 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic softness. This reduces inflation expectations and increases the probability of Fed rate cuts, driving 10-year Treasury yields lower as the market reprices the terminal rate downward.
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or pre-recession housing weakness
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 50% of 20-day windows when HOUST < 1400, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100 thousand net adds per month while FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5%, indicating the Fed's tightening is successfully cooling labor demand. This reduces inflation pressure and increases expectations for rate cuts, driving yields lower as the market reprices terminal rates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with labor market cooling
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 331 with FEDFUNDS below 4.0% indicates real rates remain accommodative despite inflation. This creates expectations for future Fed tightening, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market prices in upcoming rate increases and inflation persistence.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (both conditions) does not significantly outperform single-series CPIAUCSL signal (Rank IC difference < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 24000 billion chained 2017 dollars while retail sales (RSAFS) decline, signaling broad-based demand weakness and recession risk. This triggers safe-haven flows into Treasuries and reduces inflation expectations, compressing 10-year yields downward over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
